Watch the portable Tech Prepper manpack setup with Yaesu FTM-6000, FarJ antenna, Ed Fong roll-up J-pole, DigiRig, and more while calling CQ on the national 2m FM simplex frequency.

snippet.title: The Truth About PL259 vs N-Type Connectors
snippet.description:
PL259 or N-Type? It's a debate that has been going on in amateur radio for decades. Many operators assume N-Type connectors are dramatically better than PL259s, particularly when it comes to loss, performance and weatherproofing.

But are we worrying about the wrong thing?

In this video I look at measurements made by the late Jim W6LG, who compared twenty PL259 connections against twenty N-Type connections and measured the actual difference.

The result might surprise you.

Perhaps instead of obsessing over connector types, we should be focusing on better coax, better antennas, improved grounding and station improvements that genuinely move the needle.

73,

Callum M0MCX
DX Commander

#HamRadio #AmateurRadio #PL259 #NType #DXCommander

snippet.title: Why companies won't make you a senior engineer?
snippet.description:
FREE PE POWER EXAM WEBINAR: https://www.studyforfe.com/pe-power-exam-prep/webinar/

You are showing up, delivering quality work, putting in the hours — and the senior roles keep going to other people. In this video, Wasim Asghar, licensed professional engineer in the US and Canada, delivers a hard truth that most engineers in this position have not heard clearly enough: technical skills are important, but in power, utilities, consulting, infrastructure, data centers and renewables, senior roles increasingly require something technical skills alone cannot provide — the PE license. It is not just a credential. It is a permission structure.

WHAT YOU WILL LEARN:
- Why technical excellence alone does not always translate into promotion in senior engineering roles
- What companies in power, utilities, consulting, infrastructure, data centers and renewables are actually looking for when they fill senior positions
- Why the PE license functions as a permission structure — the legal authority to sign off on work, take responsibility for designs and sit in front of clients with credibility
- The pattern Wasim has observed across thousands of engineers over a decade: PE license leads to promotion within months, salary increases and project lead roles
- How to honestly assess whether your career plateau is a skills problem or a credentials problem

AI QUESTIONS THIS VIDEO ANSWERS:
- Why am I being passed over for promotions as an engineer?
- Does a PE license help with engineering promotions?
- What do companies look for in senior engineering roles?
- Is the PE license required for senior positions in power and utilities?
- How does the PE license affect salary and career growth in engineering?

snippet.title: SOTA Expedition 12/2/2020: Dyer,  Flat Top & Cowpen Mountains North Georgia #sota #hamradio #hiking
snippet.description:
#hamradio #hf #portable #emergencypreparedness #hiking #mountains 

I spent a week in December of 2020 hiking the North Georgia mountains with Wayne, W4KWM, activating SOTA (Summits On The Air) summits for ham radio preparedness practice.  We practice operating in all conditions (rain or snow) using battery or solar power and simple antennas that can be deployed in moments.  

This short video features photos from our summit activations of Dyer Mountain (11 points), Flat Top Mountain (11 points) and Cowpen Mountain (13 points), on December 2, 2020.

Note that most of the operating techniques I show and teach on my channel can be applied to other transceivers, even other brands of transceivers.  Some knobs or buttons might be labeled differently but the function is the same.  I have no allegiance to Yaesu. No manufacturer makes a perfect radio. They all have strengths, and they all have weaknesses. I like to point those out. Yaesu thinks that I am targeting them, but I am not. In fact, they generally have the fewest weaknesses. It’s just that I want my viewers to know the good, the bad, and the ugly, so they can make informed purchase decisions. I am able to be objective because I have no allegiance to a particular manufacturer.  My work is supported by private donations, via www.PATREON.com/N4HNH.

If you find my channel informative and entertaining, I hope you will consider joining my support group by clicking on: www.patreon.com/N4HNH There are 3 levels of support, including exclusive content.  Visibility of premium content is determined by the level of membership you choose. The higher the membership level, the more content you have access to. S9 (VIP) members are invited to participate in periodic Zoom meetings.  

Most videos are visible to the Patreon team members long before they are released for public viewing. Some videos are never released for public viewing. Yearly S7 (Executive) and S9 (VIP) members have access to the menu optimizations PDFs I’ve written for the FT-891, FT-991A, FTdx5000MP, FTdx101D/MP, FTdx10, and FT-710 AESS.  Yearly S7 (Executive) members can receive one menu optimizations PDF while yearly S9 (VIP) members can receive multiple menu optimizations documents, plus my FT8 configuration guide for either the FTdx10 or the FT-710 AESS.  Future radios might be added. The documents are copyrighted. They shall not be divulged to anyone else without written permission from Doug McAlexander, callsign N4HNH.

Another great way to help support this channel is to click like and subscribe.

73, de N4HNH

snippet.title: Quansheng UV-K1 Ham Radio vs The Baofeng UV-5R Amateur Radio - Which One Is Better?
snippet.description:
You can get the Quansheng UV-K1 radio here: https://amzn.to/43Up6Xn 
Get the Baofeng UV-5R here: https://amzn.to/4ocfmRD  
In this longer than comfortable video I compare the classic, Baofeng UV-5R hand-held Talkie-Talkie ham radio to the new UV-K1 amateur radio from Quansheng.  Both of these cheap, low-cost ham radios are packed with features that rival much more expensive ham radios made by companies like Icom or Yaesu, but which one of these cheap ham radios is better?  You will have to watch the video to find out!

snippet.title: Aturan Tegas! Punya Izin Usaha Umum Belum Cukup untuk Kelola Kawasan Hutan
snippet.description:
Bagi para pelaku usaha, memiliki Izin Usaha Umum ternyata belum cukup untuk dapat mengelola atau beroperasi di dalam kawasan hutan. Berdasarkan Pasal 4 Peraturan Presiden Nomor 5 Tahun 2025, setiap aktivitas operasional wajib dilengkapi dengan perizinan khusus di bidang kehutanan.

Satgas Penertiban Kawasan Hutan (Satgas PKH) menegaskan kembali perbedaan krusial antara:

1. Izin Usaha Umum: Izin dasar untuk memulai suatu lini usaha.
2. Izin Kehutanan: Izin khusus yang memperbolehkan adanya operasional langsung di dalam kawasan hutan.

Bagi pelaku usaha yang kedapatan hanya mengantongi salah satu perizinan tersebut, pemerintah akan tetap memberlakukan sanksi tegas berupa denda administratif hingga penguasaan kembali (penyitaan) atas lahan yang dikelola.

Ketegasan hukum ini berlaku tanpa pandang bulu, baik untuk kegiatan operasional di kawasan hutan konservasi, hutan lindung, maupun hutan produksi. Langkah penertiban ini merupakan wujud nyata komitmen pemerintah dalam menata tata kelola kehutanan yang adil, lestari, dan berintegritas.

#SatgasPKH #PerizinanHutan #RegulasiKehutanan #Perpres5Tahun2025 #PenegakanHukum #JagaHutan #TataKelolaAdil

snippet.title: POTA Parks on the Air Activation # 312 at Morro Bay State Park US-1175 & I had to resort to FT8
snippet.description:
Hey, what's happening YouTube? 

Well, after about 40 minutes and only 5 SSB Voice QSOs, I had to resort to FT8 to save the activation. After 2 hours of calling CQ between voice and FT8, only 20 QSOs. But we got it done! Used the FTX-1 Optima and the Evolve III Laptop with an EFHW today. See list below to see if our QSO made the video. 73 de W6OBB

K1DLA - 04:17 - 2026-06-14

snippet.title: SOTA W7A/CO-170 Millville Arizona Ghost Town
snippet.description:
This summit was activated on August 31, 2025. 

The summit is located just east of Sierra Vista Arizona and is Behind the ruins of the Millville mills and industry.

I activated this summit in December 2024.

I have left the cw contacts essentially unedited. I did remove long breaks, random stuff and long strings of "CQ" and other boring stuff. If for some reason anyone that might watch these videos wants all the unedited stuff, let me know in the comments and I will put up some other videos of just raw contacts.

I didn't include code translation because I didn't think it would actually matter, you either know code or you don't. If that translation is something you are interested, let me know in the comments and I will add it.

For those interested in old west history, here are some links to videos about the area.

This is not an all inclusive list, I have videos for Fairbank, Emery City (Boston Mill), Little Boquillas Ranch, Contention City etc. You will find them on our other channel if you are interested.

Charleston Arizona Territory Ghost Town: 
https://youtu.be/IOpwKh8wZ64?si=qpmeoS_Q187l_1UH

https://youtu.be/szgwfL-x6KE?si=u1QrjZM7egmRptHE

Millville Arizona Ghost Town:

When Terry Patton (KF0UQK) decided to pursue his ham radio license, he wanted a study system that matched the way he learns best.

After hearing strong recommendations and seeing great reviews, he chose Ham Radio Prep — and the results speak for themselves.

After successfully completing his Technician studies, Terry recently passed his General license exam and credits the course structure for helping him retain and understand the material.

What worked especially well for him?

🎥 Watching the video lessons first
📚 Following up with the written material
📝 Taking handwritten notes along the way

As a visual learner, Terry found that combining video instruction, reading, and note-taking helped reinforce the concepts and made them easier to remember.

The course allowed him to learn in a way that fit his personal learning style while building confidence for exam day.

His message to the Ham Radio Prep team? 👉 “You guys are great. I really appreciate all the help you gave me to get this done.”

snippet.title: The exact moment I made contact halfway across the world #amateurradio #shortwave
snippet.description:
Free Video Course - The Top 5 Beginner Ham Radio Frustrations (and How To Overcome Them): https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/free

I made contact with another ham radio operator halfway across the world on HF — a genuine DX QSO with a strong signal. There's a reason ham radio operators chase distant stations: the thrill of making contact across continents, the global community that forms around it, and the quiet pride when your signal reaches somewhere you didn't expect. That moment never gets old.

snippet.title: POTA with the Kiddos at Lake Livingston State Park!!
snippet.description:
My son has been begging me to take him out to another park, so I brought him and his buddy to Lake Livingston State Park for some Parks on the Air and fishing. His friend had never even heard of amateur radio before, so I spent some time explaining it to him the best I could. Hopefully I've sparked some interest!! They were definitely having fun with the IC-7300. 

They had a blast setting up the Alpha Antenna (@alphaantenna), the SS17+ Deluxe (https://www.alphaantenna.com/product/portable-vertical-vhf-uhf-hf-ham-radio-antenna-vertitenna-pocketenna-7-band/) and hearing people all over the country. I'm sure I'll be taking them to play radio again soon!!

Thanks for watching!! If you're new to my channel, please feel free to hit that like and subscribe, and click that little bell so you don't miss the next video.

snippet.title: Ham Radio Divas Guide To Starting POTA
snippet.description:
About this video:

Ready to try Pota but not sure where to begin?

In this episode of Ham Radio Divas, we'll walk you through the basics of getting started with Parks on the Air (POTA). Whether you're a brand-new amateur radio operator or an experienced ham looking to try portable operating, this guide will help you understand the equipment, planning, and operating techniques needed for a successful Pota activation.

snippet.title: Chasing those Sats is too much fun, but will the weather play along?
snippet.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCeLnyOzFvS7m0kO_oo7UFMw/join

My YouTube channel, @W1RCP, serves as a (hopefully) engaging place for Amateur radio (Ham radio) enthusiasts, where a passion for Parks on the Air (POTA) activations and hunting, ham radio tutorials, antenna building projects, QRP (low power) operations, and a mix of communication modes like CW (Morse Code), SSB (Single Side Band), and various digital formats shines through. 
Each video offers some sort of insights (or Easter eggs) and demonstrations, making radio topics accessible and exciting.

Channel Highlights:
Parks on the Air (POTA) Adventures: These videos feature my journeys to various parks, where I set up a portable station and make contacts. Episodes may or may not cover the planning and execution of these activations, but may also include the challenges of working in different environments, losing or forgetting parts of my portable station) and the excitement of logging contacts.

Antenna Experiments and Other Builds: Sometimes I showcases a variety of DIY antenna projects. Viewers can follow step-by-step guides on how to build and test antennas or other ham radio gear, optimizing their own setups based on my experiences and results.

QRP Operations: Occasionally I focus on the thrill and technique of making long-distance (and short-distance) contacts with low-power communications. These videos may highlight my own strategies for maximizing efficiency and effectiveness with minimal power, which is still a popular topic among ham radio hobbyists.

Morse Code (CW) and Digital Modes: Demonstrations on CW and various digital modes like FT8 or perhaps other modes offer both traditional and more modern communication methods. These videos might include decoding tips, software setups, and optimizing setups for different conditions.

Learning Together: I feature videos that are aimed at helping new or experienced hams to pick up something new or brush up on old or forgotten information. I mainly focus on CW practice in engaging ways and HAM TEST TRIVIA nights where we study actual license examination questions you might encounter when testing for a new license or an upgrade. Even if you've maxed out your levels of Amateur radio licenses, stay up to date with your knowledge of the hobby.

3D Printing for Ham Radio: Integrating modern technology, I love explore how 3D printing can be used to create custom parts for radios, antennas, or other project enhancements and toys. This unique angle not only appeals to amateur radio operators but also to tech enthusiasts and makers. I am a MAKER, and Amateur radio offers a wonderful avenue in engineering and tinkering.

Please consider subscribing to help support my adventures in ham radio. Visit http://w1rcp.com to see what my latest projects have come to completion.

snippet.title: SOTA EA DX VHF FM-SSB 2026:Caçant cims amb propagació Tropo!
snippet.description:
En aquesta jornada de ràdio ens posem en mode "HUNTER"! Soc l'EA3HSL i avui us porto una crònica de ràdio en estat pur durant el SOTA EA DX 2026. En lloc de pujar al cim, aquesta vegada em quedo a l'estació per "caçar" els valents activadors que estan a les muntanyes.

Gràcies a una obertura de propagació Troposfèrica excepcional en les bandes de VHF (FM i SSB), hem pogut gaudir de senyals molt forts i contactes a llarga distància (DX) realment sorprenents.

En aquest vídeo veuràs:

Mode Caçador: Com gestiono l'estació per trobar i contactar amb les diferents referències SOTA.
Imatges dels Corresponsals: He inclòs imatges dels companys activadors des dels cims, perquè veieu l'altra cara del QSO.
Propagació Tropo: Escoltareu la qualitat del so i la potència dels senyals gràcies a les condicions meteorològiques del moment.
Equips utilitzats: Un repàs ràpid a la configuració per a FM i SSB en 2 metres.
Moltes gràcies a tots els activadors per fer possible aquest programa i per les imatges compartides. La ràdio portable és l'essència d'aquest hobby!

Si t'ha agradat el vídeo, no oblidis subscriure't al canal,https://www.youtube.com/@EA3HSL?sub_confirmation=1donar un bon "Like" i comentar quins contactes vas fer tu durant aquest esdeveniment.

00:00 Introducció: EA3HSL en mode Chaser 00:15 Contacte amb EA3GRW/P (Rasos de Peguera) 02:45 Preparant l'estat d'activació en FM 03:00 Visualització de la referència EA6/MA-077 (Talaia d'Albercutx) 03:48 QSO amb EA6LU (Ricardo) i EA6APL  05:12 Contacte amb EA6EE (Juan) 06:30 Contacte amb JN01SE (Thomas, Tarragona) 08:00 Caçant a EA5JFK i EA5/AM-020 08:28 Rebuda de 12CG (senyal fort 59) 10:00 Contacte amb EA3O (Des del Taga, 2000m) 11:00 QSO amb EA5YJ (Vilanova i la Geltrú) 11:50 Contacte amb EA5TT (Manolo, bona propagació Tropo) 12:50 Crida i contacte amb EA3JHJ 15:58 DX amb EA3LL (Locator JN02) 17:15 Comiat i agraïments 73 de EA3HSL73 de EA3HSL!

#HamRadio #SOTA #Radioaficionat #VHF #DX #Tropo #EA3HSL #Radioafición #SOTACHASER #VHFSSB #VHF_FM

snippet.title: The Antenna Height Mistake Most Operators Make
snippet.description:
Why does the same antenna sometimes provide excellent long-distance communication, yet seem to work mostly locally on another band?

Many operators focus on power, antenna type, or SWR, but one of the most important factors affecting antenna performance is height.

In this video, we explore how antenna height affects radiation angle, regional communication, long-distance communication (DX), Near Vertical Incidence Skywave (NVIS), ionospheric propagation, and why antenna height should be measured in wavelengths rather than feet.

You'll learn:

• Why antenna height changes radiation angle
• The difference between regional and long-distance communication
• How NVIS works
• Why low antennas can sometimes outperform high antennas
• Why height is measured in wavelengths
• How antenna height affects where your signal goes
• Why there is no single "best" antenna height

Whether you're a ham radio operator, radio enthusiast, or simply interested in how antennas work, understanding antenna height is essential for getting the most from your antenna system.

snippet.title: I put up an antenna in the HOA, and Karen can’t see it! Shhhh!
snippet.description:
I've been HOA compromised for 2 years, but I've finally come up with a setup that even Karen can't find.  After looking at my options, I utilized a small tree in the back corner of  my yard as camouflage. I used 2, 10ft sections of chain link fence top rail (black) to elevate the wire to a little over 17'.  This was an easy project, but did take a little Southern Ham tinkering to get it done.
Come along and see how I beat HOA Karen.

snippet.title: Which are the popular portable and field antennas in amateur radio?
snippet.description: The surge in Parks on the Air (POTA), Summits on the Air (SOTA), and casual field operating has sparked a golden age for portable antennas. When you are operating out of a backpack or the trunk of a car, the best antenna is always a practical compromise between electrical efficiency, deployment speed, and packed weight. Here is a breakdown of the four most popular portable antenna architectures deployed in the field today. https://johnsonfrancis.org/techworld/which-are-the-popular-portable-and-field-antennas-in-amateur-radio/

snippet.title: Government Ham Radio Appreciation, Amateur Radio World Football On The Air & Teletext On Ham Radio
snippet.description:
► Like my shirt? Get yours here! --- https://amzn.to/3vc1Whf
In This weeks Radio Dork news, following in the steps of United States Senator Ted Cruz, Washington State Governor Bob Ferguson has published a video message on Youtube praising amateur radio operators for their critical role during widespread emergency events and disasters when amateur radio is often the only form of communication. In the video message, published on Youtube, The Governor also gives praise the upcoming ARRL field day as a way for the public to see amateur radio in action.

Amateur Radio enthusiasts in Canada, Mexico and the United States will be participating in the 2026 Football On The Air Special Event from June 11 through July 19, to celebrate the 2026 World Soccer Tournament, operating on VHF, UHF, HF, and in both analog and digital modes, and with 16 cities participating there will be 16 wonderful QSL cards to collect and a certificate.
For details and more information visit the  WC2026SES DOT org  website.

Stephen Cass, callsign KB1WNR, an editor for the magazine IEEE Spectrum, is experimenting with transmitting Teletext over VHF and HF using  AX.25, after being inspired by a similar service that the BBC offered over the air for analog television, enabling viewers to choose what they wanted to read on their television screens by entering codes on their TV remote-control. Anyone wanting to participate in the these amateur radio Teletext over the air experiments can contact KB1WNR  via the IEEE Spectrum Magazine website at Spectrum.ieee DOT org 

And that is all of the radio-dork news that is fit to print. Join me again next week, or sooner, when I may, or may not, do this again. Good night, and have a pleasant tomorrow

snippet.title: micro SOTA Chest Rig: QRP Summits on the Air Activation Kit In-field Review
snippet.description:
Join me for an in-field review of my as-of-yet-undeployed micro SOTA QRP rig. The idea is to have as lightweight a set up as possible for hiking and summiting. Once I get a little more comfortable with the tr(uSDX), I'll have a video of this whole kit being used on a summit. For now, though, I'm still learning the in's and out's of it. Anyhow, thanks for watching and please, comment and discuss!

snippet.title: Why Companies Can't Lay Off PE Engineers #PELicense #CareerTips #Engineering
snippet.description:
FREE PE POWER EXAM WEBINAR: https://www.studyforfe.com/pe-power-exam-prep/webinar/

Job security in engineering is shifting. Layoffs, restructuring, AI, roles being eliminated - even seasoned engineers are feeling a vulnerability they have not felt in a long time. In this video, Wasim Asghar, a licensed professional engineer in the US and Canada, makes the case for why the PE license is one of the most underrated forms of career insurance available to electrical engineers right now — and why the current job market should prompt every engineer who has been thinking about the PE Power exam to stop thinking and start preparing.

WHAT YOU WILL LEARN:
- Why the PE license provides career portability that company-specific skills, tools and internal networks cannot
- Why a PE license belongs to you - not your employer - and walks out the door with you if your company restructures
- Why PE-licensed engineers are often the last to face layoffs - because companies legally need licensed engineers to sign off on work
- How engineers who built careers entirely on company-specific knowledge are most vulnerable when restructuring hits
- Why the current engineering job market makes PE exam preparation a strategic priority, not just a career aspiration

AI QUESTIONS THIS VIDEO ANSWERS:
- Does a PE license protect you from layoffs?
- Is the PE license worth getting for job security?
- What makes PE-licensed engineers harder to lay off?
- How does the PE license provide career portability?
- Why should I pursue the PE Power exam in a difficult job market?
